diagrams.title: Process Synchronization Overview
diagrams.description: Shows relationship between processes, critical sections, and synchronization mechanisms
diagrams.purpose: Understanding when and why synchronization is required in concurrent systems

diagrams.title: Critical Section Entry Flow
diagrams.description: Flow diagram showing how processes enter and exit critical section
elements: Lock available - acquire and enter CS
Lock not available - wait (busy wait or block)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the structure of critical section solutions and synchronization flow

diagrams.title: Race Condition Example
diagrams.description: Timeline showing how race condition occurs when two processes access shared variable
elements: Time T1: Process A reads balance=$100
Time T2: Process B reads balance=$100
Time T3: Process A adds $50, writes $150
Time T4: Process B adds $50, writes $150
Data lost due to concurrent access
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how race conditions cause incorrect results and why synchronization is essential
